legend.notes: Free-form. Empty when crystal clear. Otherwise names the gotcha, the issue number, or the unmet condition.

aider.notes: Markdown transcript discovered lazily per cwd; row appears after the first user message lands in .aider.chat.history.md — recorded + validated.

opencode.notes: SQLite-backed; no PID binding required

aider.notes: idle-flush turn-end adds ~5s before ready settles; markdown file persists after exit

claudecode.notes: clean exit + SIGKILL OK; crash mid-turn covered indirectly via sweep. #321 zombie-on-restart fixed in 0.4.x — recipe now passes 11/11 phases.

codex.notes: All 3 variants recorded via drive-codex-interactive.sh's sigkill + restart primitives (clean Ctrl-D exit / SIGKILL idle / SIGKILL mid-turn); expected.jsonl validates 14/14 phases. sigkill targets the daemon's PID directly (rollout's lsof write-FD holder, per codex/pid.go) since codex's uniform argv has no per-session marker. Variant 3 sleeps 5 s before the kill to clear codex's ~2.7 s ready→working promotion lag so the mid-turn working state is captured.

opencode.notes: #296 ghost sessions when no process; #278 ~2.5s tail latency on working→ready

claudecode.notes: PID liveness check holds session past readyTTL

opencode.notes: no PID binding — liveness relies on DB watcher signal

aider.notes: aider re-loads chat history from .aider.chat.history.md but no formal session-id concept

claudecode.notes: --continue / --resume; transcript appended

codex.notes: /resume command + picker (2026)

opencode.notes: capabilities.json declares yes; ParentSessionID not populated recursively for resume chains

aider.notes: aider has no /clear analogue; restart creates new history file

claudecode.notes: /clear rotates UUID; daemon emits transcript_removed for old UUID via PIDManager's same-PID cleanup (commit 56c4667 / issue #169). ~50ms overlap between new session's transcript_new and old session's transcript_removed is bounded by spec at 1s. Eliminating overlap entirely conflicts with multi-session-same-cwd support.

codex.notes: /new (or /clear) starts a fresh session that supersedes the old one. Daemon (Source=FilesUnderRoot ~/.codex/sessions) detects the new rollout as a brand-new session_id in ready — the complete reset signal. Both rollouts share one PID, so when the new rollout's PID is discovered the daemon's same-PID cleanup (#169, pid_manager.go:373-399) removes the old session and emits transcript_removed — the SAME path as claudecode, ~7ms after the new pid_discovered (NOT a readyTTL idle sweep). No old_session_ended phase is asserted: a fixture scoping choice (v2_session_birth is the canonical reset proxy, and reset is lazy so the removal isn't anchored to the keystroke), not a codex limitation. Recorded via the reset_session step (both rollout session_ids captured); expected.jsonl 8/8.

opencode.notes: recorded — live-TUI reset_session (/new); the prior gap:slash-command was stale (the live-REPL driver landed in #495).

aider.notes: /undo reverts the last aider git commit; /reset wipes files + chat; /clear clears chat history. No multi-point checkpoint picker. The /undo path surfaces as a normal chat turn in the transcript and a git HEAD movement — distinguishable from a regular turn only via the slash-command marker, not via session_id change.

claudecode.notes: /rewind (alias /checkpoint, also Esc+Esc) restores files/conversation per prompt — recorded, 8/8 phases pass.

codex.notes: No native checkpoint-rewind as of v0.130 (May 2026): no /rewind command, no file-edit checkpoint restore; the experimental /undo was removed (#9618). codex HAS /fork (conversation fork → new linked session_id, original intact) and TUI Esc-Esc backtrack, but that is branching, not file+conversation rollback — do NOT re-mark this `yes` on the strength of /fork. Open feature requests #11626 + #12558 model after Claude Code. Reassess on next major Codex release.

opencode.notes: driver+daemon ready, but the send->send->/undo->send recording hits a run_live slash->send composer hand-off race under the slow local model (4 attempts); not recordable on this model.

pi.notes: Stock Pi CLI has no rewind (pi-mono discussion #1223). Third-party `pi-rewind` extension exists at arpagon/pi-rewind but isn't part of the default install, so irrlicht's observation target — stock Pi — has nothing to record.

claudecode.notes: Cloud variants exist (web, Routines, GHA action, --remote/--teleport) but write no local transcript and expose no read API — FilesUnderRoot+pgrep transport cannot observe them.

codex.notes: Codex Cloud exists; irrlicht has no API-based watcher

opencode.notes: opencode sessions are a local ProcessOwnedStore (SQLite keyed to a local process + cwd); no cloud/hosted background-agent concept to observe

aider.notes: idle-flush turn-end (~5s effective latency on working→ready)

opencode.notes: recorded 2026-05 (4/4)

aider.notes: tool calls reduced to '> Applied edit' / '> Running' markdown markers; coarse-grained

opencode.notes: recorded 2026-05 (4/4); gemma emitted a real write tool call inside the working span

aider.notes: no task_list capability

claudecode.notes: recorded; TodoWrite → TaskDeltas

codex.notes: no task_list capability

opencode.notes: recorded 2026-05; todowrite → TaskDeltas

aider.notes: aider retries across turns rather than within; idle-flush splits attempts

opencode.notes: recorded 2026-05 (4/4); write→read→edit in one contiguous working span (daemon was unknown, proven full)

claudecode.notes: /help, /cost, /status keep the session ready — recorded, 4/4 phases pass.

opencode.notes: recorded — /help via the live-TUI slash step; a no-LLM local command writes zero parts so the session stays ready (no working flicker).

aider.notes: idle-flush windows compound on long sessions

claudecode.notes: #102 flicker closed; long multi-turn sessions stable — recorded, 9/9 phases pass.

codex.notes: #105 stale-tool timer flicker (closed); task_complete dependence creates fallback risk

opencode.notes: recorded — prior daemon=bug was a mislabel; routed onto the long-lived run_live path (live marker) so the final settle is captured instead of truncated by the headless per-run process-exit race (#492).

aider.notes: architect mode does limited multi-step but not continuous self-prompt

claudecode.notes: /goal slash command (self-prompt); session holds working across internal iterations — recorded, 6/6 phases pass. Background-loop nuances remain partial.

codex.notes: Codex /goal command (Ralph loop)

opencode.notes: stock opencode has no autonomous self-prompting/goal loop — turns end at session.idle awaiting the next user message; self-prompting is plugin-only (oh-my-opencode); upstream /loop request still open

claudecode.notes: /goal with iteration cap resolves to ready cleanly at the cap — recorded, 6/6 phases pass.

codex.notes: /goal has caps

opencode.notes: presupposes the autonomous-loop primitive stock opencode lacks → no max-iterations cap to observe

aider.notes: LLM error blockquote treated as turn_done (#262); quota errors land in same path

claudecode.notes: 5-hour budget end resolves state cleanly — recorded, 5/5 phases pass.

opencode.notes: Two distinct blockers verified live with mock-openai-429: (1) a 429 is retried indefinitely by the AI-SDK (never persists message.data.error); (2) a non-retryable 403 DOES persist as message.data.error (APIError isRetryable=false) but on a ZERO-PART assistant message, so the watcher.go scanParts JOIN (watcher.go:482-489) cannot reach msgData/isErrorMessage — the narrow zero-part edge at watcher.go:521-527 is the load-bearing blocker. Records when the watcher broadens to scan messages with .data.error and no terminal part, or when opencode emits a step-start before the API error fires.

aider.notes: TUI-based, no queue concept

claudecode.notes: Queued message keeps state working through the turn — recorded, 4/4 phases pass.

opencode.notes: recorded — a message typed mid-turn (mid_turn_send) is queued by the TUI (not written to SQLite until consumed) so the daemon stays working with no flicker, then runs as the next turn.

aider.notes: /yes-always is blanket, not classifier-based

claudecode.notes: Auto Mode (shipped Mar 2026); PermissionPending path covers waiting on risky calls — recorded, 4/4 phases pass.

opencode.notes: recorded — a config-allowed (permission.bash=allow, per-run settings blob) bash tool auto-executes with no prompt; ready->working->ready, no waiting.

claudecode.notes: /compact runs as a turn; transitions should stay working then ready — not specifically validated

opencode.notes: recorded — /summarize via the live-TUI slash step; the summarization turn drives a working->ready arc on the same session.

pi.notes: Compaction events emitted as bare 'assistant' (not turn-done); risk of working→ready→working flicker per exploration

aider.notes: idle-flush is the de-facto terminal signal; ~5s lag

claudecode.notes: stop_reason=end_turn handled

codex.notes: task_complete handled

opencode.notes: recorded 2026-05 (4/4); step-finish reason=stop

aider.notes: #262 fixed — error blockquote emits turn_done early

claudecode.notes: Turn-level error keeps state working then ready — recorded, 4/4 phases pass.

opencode.notes: recorded — a reason=length abort (output overflows the context window) emits a step-start then a terminal step-finish reason=length, which parser.go maps to turn_done and the daemon/replay both observe (no message.data.error carrier needed). Driven on the long-lived run_live path.

aider.notes: /run command available; observability folds into '> Running' tool marker

claudecode.notes: #62 closed; ! prefix handled; BashExecution events skipped

opencode.notes: no user-level `!cmd` shell escape outside a model turn; shell is the model-invoked bash tool, already covered by auto-executed-tool-call

aider.notes: RawLineParser; behavior on very long lines unvalidated

claudecode.notes: #270 stuck-working fix; oversized lines handled — recorded, 3/3 phases pass.

opencode.notes: applicable:false / blocked — opencode caps tool output (read tool → 2000 chars; bash → ~30-50KB preview with overflow to a tool-output/ sidecar), so a single >=256KB transcript line cannot be induced via opencode tools

aider.notes: aider can ask via /ask interactively but no JSONL-style tool marker; not detected

claudecode.notes: AskUserQuestion detected explicitly; #150 + #307 fswatcher/JSONL race scenarios fixed

codex.notes: no question tool in schema

opencode.notes: no AskUserQuestion-style structured blocking-question primitive that surfaces a daemon-observable waiting state (SQLite part schema = step-start/step-finish/text/tool only)

aider.notes: plan_mode declared in capabilities.json but not detected in parser

claudecode.notes: ExitPlanMode tool detected; #152 fixed

codex.notes: #322 OPEN — <proposed_plan> turns end as 'ready' instead of 'waiting'; #325 OPEN — dismissal leaves session stuck in waiting

opencode.notes: opencode Plan mode is a tool-restriction (read-only) mode, not a blocking approval gate; no daemon-observable waiting→approval transition (parser/watcher have no waiting/ExitPlanMode branch)

aider.notes: aider's confirm prompts are stdin/TUI; not visible to irrlicht via transcript

claudecode.notes: PreToolUse/PostToolUse hooks wired via HTTP receiver; #204 closed (late hook bounce-back)

codex.notes: capabilities.json: permission_hooks: false

opencode.notes: frozen — a held tool gate leaves no daemon-observable trace: opencode persists permission state in an ephemeral table the watcher never reads (it reads only part/message/session); the gated call shows only as a normal open tool call.

aider.notes: Ctrl-C in aider TUI; abort markdown signature may differ from claude ESC pattern

claudecode.notes: tool-denial + ESC marker recognized; #13 fixed

opencode.notes: interrupt step ported + daemon parses reason=interrupted, but the local gemma model never surfaces a reason=interrupted step-finish for a TUI ESC (stalls then finishes on stop, 4 attempts); not elicitable on this provider.

aider.notes: idle-flush may fire during slow remote model calls causing premature ready

codex.notes: task_complete fallback to assistant_message risks mid-stream flicker

opencode.notes: recorded 2026-05 (5/5); WAL-fsnotify watcher held working ~110s, no flicker

claudecode.notes: file-based child transcripts; ParentSessionID inferred from filesystem; #133/#134 stop_reason=null fixed; #88 closed

codex.notes: capabilities.json: subagents: false

opencode.notes: recorded — a Task-tool subagent persists session.parent_id; the daemon emits parent_linked (SQLite-store spawn proof, no child .jsonl).

claudecode.notes: unified rule: session held working while bg child alive; child counted in SubagentCount; reevaluateParent/hasActiveChildren + ComputeSubagentSummary already satisfy the signal

opencode.notes: opencode subagents are FOREGROUND task-tool children the parent awaits (foreground-subagent recorded); no documented fire-and-forget background/async dispatch, and the headless driver can't observe a concurrent mid-turn child

claudecode.notes: #445/#450 + 265ebf22: parser snapshots the Bash run_in_background backgroundTaskId into BackgroundProcessCount and holds the session working past end_turn; a terminal <task-notification> (the SDK-harness completion shape) drops the process so it settles working→ready once the bg process exits. Recorded + validated 5/5.

codex.notes: shell tool can spawn background process; not tracked

opencode.notes: opencode's bash tool runs synchronously to completion; no run_in_background dispatch + later-completion event observable via the ProcessOwnedStore transport (MetricsReader never sets BackgroundProcessCount)

claudecode.notes: deleteWithChildren + orphan transcript predicates

opencode.notes: recorded — sigkill orphans the PID-bound parent+child (the subagent is a child session of the same opencode process); the daemon reaps both and the #321 no-spurious-transition invariant holds.

aider.notes: aider uses a single .aider.chat.history.md per cwd; two aiders in same cwd would race that file

claudecode.notes: Per-UUID transcripts; pid.go is metadata-first (#60/#109/#169) so no PID-swap flap. Recorded via the new start_session driver primitive — two concurrent same-cwd sessions, 7/7 expected phases pass.

opencode.notes: recorded known_failing — identity isolation holds (two distinct ses_ in one cwd, no flap/merge), but the daemon emits ZERO working transitions for concurrent headless same-cwd sessions (each opencode run exits before the SQLite flush). Filed daemon bug #503.

claudecode.notes: claudecode + codex/pi/aider/opencode coexistence — adapter labels are independent

opencode.notes: recorded — prior daemon=bug was a mislabel; 5 adapters coexist in one cwd as independent working->ready arcs, no merge/flap (opencode keyed by SQLite session.id).

aider.notes: regex-parsed '> Tokens: N sent, M received'; no cache-bucket distinction

opencode.notes: recorded — prior daemon=bug was a mislabel; token extraction + monotonic accumulation are full (the over-specified third per-turn state arc was de-pinned: a transport WAL-poll coalescing limit, not a token defect).

Recipe authored (eea2f166); records when a viable second model exists.

claudecode.notes: Multiple provider backends (Anthropic/Vertex/Bedrock) chosen at startup; no documented mid-turn fallback chain and provider-routed model name is identical. Turn-level state arc is observable; failover-qua-failover is not. conf 0.55.
